message:
feat(cli): add redaction rules to support-bundle (bd-31ps.2.2)

Adds regex-based redaction of sensitive data in support bundles:
- API keys (sk-*), AWS keys (AKIA*), GitHub tokens, Vault tokens,
  Slack tokens, Bearer headers, JWTs, and generic key=value secrets
- Redaction enabled by default; --no-redact flag with warning
- Manifest JSON includes redaction summary (enabled, files_modified,
  pattern names)
- Safe values (git SHAs, timestamps, versions) not redacted

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Users can discover the page and understand its purpose.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:38:11.908039544Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:38:11.908039544Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.10.4","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:38:11.908039544Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31ps.2","title":"Add acfs support-bundle command (diagnostics pack)","description":"## Background
Support/debugging requires scattered manual steps (logs, state.json, doctor output, versions). A single support bundle simplifies triage and reduces back-and-forth.

## Goal
Add an `acfs support-bundle` command that collects diagnostics into a timestamped archive with redaction.

## Scope
- New script in scripts/lib/support.sh.
- New CLI entrypoint via acfs command dispatcher.
- Bundle includes: state.json, install logs, doctor --json, versions, OS info, selected config files, recent errors.

## Considerations
- Redact tokens/secrets (API keys, auth tokens).
- Size limits and opt-in for large logs.
- Output should be safe to share.

## Testing
- Unit tests for redaction + file collection.
- Docker E2E: create bundle and verify contents.

## Acceptance Criteria
- One command produces a shareable bundle with manifest + logs + doctor JSON.
- Redaction verified and documented.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:17.754579012Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:17.754579012Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:17.754579012Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31ps.2.1","title":"Support-bundle implementation + CLI wiring","description":"## Goal
Implement the support-bundle collection logic and CLI entrypoint.

## Tasks
- Create scripts/lib/support.sh with functions: collect_files, capture_doctor_json, capture_versions, capture_env_summary.
- Add `acfs support-bundle` command routing (acfs wrapper / doctor.sh dispatch table).
- Output bundle directory: ~/.acfs/support/<timestamp>/ plus .tar.gz archive.
- Include a manifest JSON describing included files and versions.

## Considerations
- Ensure stdout stays clean for piping; write progress to stderr via logging.sh.
- Avoid requiring root; use sudo only when needed for logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Running `acfs support-bundle` produces a tarball + manifest JSON.
- Bundle includes doctor JSON and state.json when present.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:37.265721256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T22:40:31.376687322Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T22:40:31.376618843Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:37.265721256Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:41:52.853108258Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:42:22.564866490Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Added --no-redact flag, redaction summary in manifest JSON.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:55.389196201Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31ps.2.3","title":"Tests: acfs support-bundle","description":"## Goal
Add automated coverage for support-bundle generation.

## Tasks
- Unit tests for collection functions (mock files + redaction).
- Docker E2E: run `acfs support-bundle`, verify tarball contains expected files + manifest JSON.
- Log output to /tmp/acfs_support_bundle_test_TIMESTAMP.log with pass/fail summary.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests fail if required bundle components are missing.
- E2E run produces detailed logs + summary.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:23:05.075169407Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:42:51.658165458Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:23:05.075169407Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:42:39.178930848Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:42:51.657404163Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31ps.2.4","title":"Docs: support-bundle usage + privacy","description":"## Goal
Document support-bundle usage and privacy expectations.

## Tasks
- Add README section describing `acfs support-bundle` and where output is stored.
- Include redaction notes + how to disable with --no-redact.
- Add troubleshooting guidance on what to attach when filing issues.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can find and run the command easily.
- Clear warning about sensitive data and redaction behavior.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:23:22.919954847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:23:22.919954847Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:23:22.919954847Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31ps.3","title":"Structured install logging + JSON summary artifacts","description":"## Background
Install failures are hard to diagnose without durable logs. We want deterministic artifacts: a log file and a JSON summary of phases, durations, and failures.

## Goal
Add structured install logging (log file + JSON summary) without polluting stdout.

## Scope
- Default log file under ~/.acfs/logs/install_YYYYmmdd_HHMMSS.log.
- JSON summary artifact with phase timings, failed step, and resume hints.
- Redaction for sensitive values in logs.

## Considerations
- Respect --quiet and --dry-run behaviors.
- Preserve stderr progress output.

## Testing
- Unit tests for summary JSON and redaction.
- Integration test in tests/vm to assert artifacts exist.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every install run produces a log file and summary JSON.
- Summary is stable and parseable for tooling.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:23:49.243580242Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:23:49.243580242Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.3","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:23:49.243580242Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

@@ -29,7 +29,9 @@ fi
 # Configuration
 # ============================================================
 VERBOSE=false
+REDACT=true
 OUTPUT_BASE="${ACFS_HOME}/support"
+REDACTION_COUNT=0
 
 # ============================================================
 # Parse arguments
@@ -44,14 +46,20 @@ while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
             OUTPUT_BASE="$2"
             shift 2
             ;;
+        --no-redact)
+            REDACT=false
+            shift
+            ;;
         --help|-h)
             echo "Usage: acfs support-bundle [options]"
             echo ""
             echo "Collect diagnostic data into a tarball for troubleshooting."
+            echo "Sensitive data (API keys, tokens, secrets) is redacted by default."
             echo ""
             echo "Options:"
             echo "  --verbose, -v    Show detailed output during collection"
             echo "  --output, -o DIR Output directory (default: ~/.acfs/support)"
+            echo "  --no-redact      Disable secret redaction (WARNING: bundle may contain secrets)"
             echo "  --help, -h       Show this help"
             echo ""
             echo "Output:"
@@ -276,17 +284,98 @@ write_manifest() {
         --arg bundle_dir "$(basename "$bundle_dir")" \
         --argjson files "$files_json" \
         --argjson file_count "${#BUNDLE_FILES[@]}" \
+        --argjson redaction_enabled "$( [[ "$REDACT" == "true" ]] && echo true || echo false )" \
+        --argjson redaction_files_modified "$REDACTION_COUNT" \
         '{
             schema_version: $schema_version,
             created_at: $created_at,
             created_by: $created_by,
             acfs_version: $acfs_version,
             bundle_id: $bundle_dir,
             file_count: $file_count,
-            files: $files
+            files: $files,
+            redaction: {
+                enabled: $redaction_enabled,
+                files_modified: $redaction_files_modified,
+                patterns: ["api_key", "aws_key", "github_token", "github_pat", "vault_token", "slack_token", "bearer", "jwt", "password", "generic_secret"]
+            }
         }' > "$manifest_file" 2>/dev/null || return 1
 }
 
+# ============================================================
+# Redaction
+# ============================================================
+
+# Redact sensitive values from a single text file in-place.
+# Increments REDACTION_COUNT for each substitution made.
+# Usage: redact_file <file_path>
+redact_file() {
+    local file="$1"
+
+    # Skip binary files (check first 512 bytes for null bytes)
+    if head -c 512 "$file" 2>/dev/null | grep -qP '\x00'; then
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    # Count lines before redaction for diff
+    local before_hash
+    before_hash=$(md5sum "$file" 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $1}') || return 0
+
+    # Apply redaction patterns using sed -E (extended regex)
+    # Order: specific patterns first, then generic catch-alls
+    sed -E -i \
+        -e 's/sk-[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{20,}/<REDACTED:api_key>/g' \
+        -e 's/AKIA[A-Z0-9]{16}/<REDACTED:aws_key>/g' \
+        -e 's/ghp_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36,}/<REDACTED:github_token>/g' \
+        -e 's/ghs_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36,}/<REDACTED:github_token>/g' \
+        -e 's/github_pat_[a-zA-Z0-9_]{22,}/<REDACTED:github_pat>/g' \
+        -e 's/hvs\.[a-zA-Z0-9]{20,}/<REDACTED:vault_token>/g' \
+        -e 's/xox[bpsar]-[a-zA-Z0-9-]{10,}/<REDACTED:slack_token>/g' \
+        -e 's/Bearer [a-zA-Z0-9._\/-]{10,}/Bearer <REDACTED:bearer>/g' \
+        -e 's/eyJ[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{10,}\.[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{10,}\.[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{10,}/<REDACTED:jwt>/g' \
+        "$file" 2>/dev/null || return 0
+
+    # Generic key=value secrets (case-insensitive would need per-line processing;
+    # instead match common casings)
+    sed -E -i \
+        -e 's/(api_key|API_KEY|ApiKey|api_secret|API_SECRET|secret_key|SECRET_KEY|access_token|ACCESS_TOKEN|refresh_token|REFRESH_TOKEN|auth_token|AUTH_TOKEN|client_secret|CLIENT_SECRET|private_key|PRIVATE_KEY)([=:]["'"'"']?)([^ "'"'"'\n]{8,})/\1\2<REDACTED:\1>/g' \
+        -e 's/(password|PASSWORD|passwd|PASSWD)([=:]["'"'"']?)([^ "'"'"'\n]{4,})/\1\2<REDACTED:password>/g' \
+        "$file" 2>/dev/null || return 0
+
+    # Check if file changed
+    local after_hash
+    after_hash=$(md5sum "$file" 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $1}') || return 0
+    if [[ "$before_hash" != "$after_hash" ]]; then
+        ((REDACTION_COUNT++))
+    fi
+}
+
+# Walk all files in the bundle directory and apply redaction.
+# Usage: redact_bundle <bundle_dir>
+redact_bundle() {
+    local bundle_dir="$1"
+
+    if [[ "$REDACT" != "true" ]]; then
+        log_warn "Redaction disabled (--no-redact). Bundle may contain secrets."
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    log_detail "Redacting sensitive data..."
+
+    local file_count=0
+    while IFS= read -r file; do
+        redact_file "$file"
+        ((file_count++))
+    done < <(find "$bundle_dir" -type f \( \
+        -name '*.json' -o -name '*.log' -o -name '*.txt' \
+        -o -name '*.yaml' -o -name '*.yml' -o -name '*.sh' \
+        -o -name '*.zshrc' -o -name '.zshrc' \
+        -o -name 'os-release' -o -name 'VERSION' \
+        \) 2>/dev/null)
+
+    [[ "$VERBOSE" == "true" ]] && log_detail "Scanned $file_count files, redacted $REDACTION_COUNT"
+}
+
 # ============================================================
 # Main bundle collection
 # ============================================================
@@ -372,6 +461,9 @@ main() {
         collect_file "$ACFS_HOME/acfs.manifest.yaml" "$bundle_dir/config" "acfs.manifest.yaml"
     fi
 
+    # --- Redact sensitive data ---
+    redact_bundle "$bundle_dir"
+
     # --- Write manifest ---
     log_detail "Writing manifest..."
     write_manifest "$bundle_dir"
